reason: Both answers converge on the same core recommendation (contractor bridge + migrate to managed K8s + don't just replace the DevOps hire), and neither contains factual errors. The differentiator is coverage of the key points: Answer B explicitly hits all four — contractor to stabilize, avoiding a single replacement hire (proposing shared responsibility across existing engineers instead), migration to managed K8s with correct reasoning about reduced toil, and explicitly flagging that 40 services for 10 engineers is a consolidation/simplification opportunity worth revisiting long-term. Answer A misses the consolidation point entirely, and its final suggestion to "hire one platform-minded engineer... to own infra part-time" is in mild tension with its own stated lesson about bus factor, even if partially softened by mentioning shared on-call. Answer B is also more concise while covering more ground, with no padding.
